Name: Sales
Description: Income from any normal business activity

Name: Rental Income
Description: Residential rental income

Name: Dividends Received
Description: Dividend income

Name: Other Revenue
Description: Any other income that does not relate to normal business activities and is not recurring

Name: Government Grants
Description: A grant is a contribution, gift, or subsidy (in cash or kind) bestowed to the entity (grantee) by a government or other organisation (grantor) for specified purposes. Grants are usually conditional upon certain qualifications such as its use and maintenance of specified standards.

Name: Interest Income
Description: Interest income

Name: Capital Gain/(Loss) on Disposal of Asset
Gain/(Loss) on Sale of Non-Current Asset
Non Taxable Income
Description: Any income received that is exempt from tax.

Name: Opening Stock
Description: Opening stock levels

Name: Opening WIP
Description: Opening Work In Progress

Name: Closing WIP
Description: Closing Work In Progress

Name: Closing Stock
Description: Closing stock levels

Name: Bank Fees
Description: Fees charged by your bank for transactions regarding your bank account(s).

Name: Borrowing Costs Amortised
Description: Borrowing cost amortised for the year.

Name: Cleaning & Laundry
Description: Expenses incurred for cleaning business property.

Name: Computer Expenses
Description: Computer Expenses that are less than $300.00

Name: Depreciation
Description: The amount of the asset''s cost (based on the useful life) that was consumed during the period

Name: Freight & Courier
Description: Expenses incurred on courier & freight costs

Name: Hire of Plant & Equipment
Description: Interest expenses incurred for finance leases

Name: Operating Lease Payments
Description: Include operating lease payments which are required to be disclosed separately to rental lease payments

Name: Impairment
Description: An impairment loss arises when the carrying amount of an asset exceeds its recoverable amount. If appropriate, the recoverable amount can be established by discounting future net cash flows expected to be received from that asset''s use. An impairment loss reduces an overstated carrying amount (book value) to fair value.

Name: Insurance
Description: Expenses incurred for insuring the business'' assets

Name: Interest Expense
Description: Any interest expenses paid to business bank accounts or credit card accounts.

Name: Legal Expenses
Description: Expenses incurred on any legal matters

Name: Filing Fees
Description: ASIC Registration Fees

Name: Fines & Penalties
Description: Expenses incurred from fines and penalties incurred by the business

Name: Electricity & Gas
Description: Expenses incurred for lighting, powering or heating the premises

Name: Office Expenses
Description: General expenses related to the running of the business office.

Name: Printing & Stationery
Description: Expenses incurred by the entity as a result of printing and stationery

Name: Rent
Description: The payment to lease a building or area.

Name: Repairs & Maintenance
Description: Expenses incurred on a damaged or run down asset that will bring the asset back to its original condition.

Name: Wages & Salaries
Description: Payment to employees in exchange for their resources

Name: Superannuation
Description: Superannuation contributions

Name: Subscriptions & Memberships
Description: E.g. Magazines, professional bodies.

Name: Telephone
Description: Expenditure incurred from any business-related phone calls, phone lines, or internet connections

Name: Internet
Description: Expenditure incurred from any business-related phone calls, phone lines, or internet connections

Name: Mobile Phone
Description: Expenditure incurred from any business-related phone calls, phone lines, or internet connections

Name: Travel - National
Description: Expenses incurred from domestic travel which has a business purpose

Name: Travel - International
Description: Expenses incurred from international travel which has a business purpose

Name: Non Recurring Items
Description: Non-recurring items are those items of revenue or expense which derive from events or transactions that are not expected to occur frequently, are distinct from the ordinary operations of the entity, and are outside the control or influence of managers or owners.

Name: Other Non Deductible Items
Description: Include other expenses which are not allowed as a tax deduction

Name: Bank Account (Xero Ledger)
Description: Use this instead of system account to allow manual journals

Name: Accounts Receivable (Xero Ledger)
Description: Use this instead of system account to allow manual journals

Name: Accounts Receivable
Description: Outstanding invoices the company has issued out to the client but has not yet received in cash at balance date.

Name: Accounts Receivable - Provision for Doubtful Debts
Prepayments
Description: An expenditure that has been paid for in advance.

Name: Work In Progress
Description: Materials and components that have begun their transformation to finished products, but remain unfinished at period end.

Name: Land at Cost
Description: Include total cost of land, do not include any revaluation differential

Name: Land Revaluation
Description: Include the amount by which land has been revalued, do not include the original/historical cost of the land.

Name: Building at Cost
Description: Buildings include marina berths and hangars.

Name: Stamp Duty
Description: Stamp Duty on Building

Name: Less Accumulated Depreciation on Structural Improvements
Description: Accumulated depreciation of buildings

Name: Building Revaluation
Description: Include the revaluation differntial only, exlcude the historical cost of building

Name: Less Accumulated Amortisation of Leasehold Improvements
Office Equipment
Description: Office equipment that is owned and controlled by the business

Name: Less Accumulated Depreciation on Office Equipment
Description: The total amount of office equipment cost that has been consumed by the entity (based on the useful life)

Name: Computer Equipment
Description: Computer equipment that is owned and controlled by the business

Name: Less Accumulated Depreciation on Computer Equipment
Description: The total amount of computer equipment cost that has been consumed by the business (based on the useful life)

Name: Motor Vehicles
Description: Motor Vehicles that are owned and controlled by the business

Name: Less Accumulated Depreciation on Motor Vehicles
Description: The total amount of motor vehicle cost that has been consumed by the business (based on the useful life)

Name: Plant & Equipment
Description: Plant owned, Equipment owned & Loose tools owned (pooled cost greater than $500)

Name: Less Accumulated Depreciation on Plant & Equipment
Description: The total amount of Plant & Equipment cost that has been consumed by the business (based on the useful life)

Name: Intangibles
Description: Intangible assets are non physical in form yet they provide benefits (future service potential) to the entity or give some kind of advantage in the marketplace.

Name: Intangibles - Amortisation
Description: Amortisation of intangibles

Name: Goodwill
Description: Goodwill is the amount by which the purchase price for a business combination exceeds the value of the identifiable assets, liabilities and contingent liabilities at the date of acquisition. Goodwill usually represents the reputation and customer base that the acquired business has developed whilst in operation.

Name: Less Accumulated Impairment of Goodwill
Description: Include the accumulated impairment of goodwill. This represents the total impairment that has taken place up to period end on goodwill.

Name: Research & Development
Description: Research and development are costs incurred to discover new knowledge about products, processes, and services, in order to create new and improved products, processes, and services that meet market needs.

Name: Less Accumulated Impairment on Research & Development
Description: Include the accumulated impairment of research and development. This represents the total impairment that has taken place up to period end on research and development.

Name: Accounts Payable
Description: Outstanding invoices the company has received from suppliers but has not yet paid at balance date

Name: Accounts Payable (Xero Ledger)
Description: Use this instead of system account to allow manual journals

Name: Unpaid Expense Claims
Description: Expense claims typically made by employees/shareholder employees still outstanding.

Name: Wages Payable
Description: Xero automatically updates this account for payroll entries created using Payroll and will store the payroll amount to be paid to the employee for the pay run. This account enables you to maintain separate accounts for employee Wages Payable amounts and Accounts Payable amounts

Name: Deferred Income
Description: Deferred income is income the entity has received prior to selling a product or providing a service.

Name: Provisions
Description: A provision is a liability uncertain as to timing or amount. Include all other provisions not otherwise separately categorised

Name: GST
Description: The balance in this account represents GST owing to or from the ATO. At the end of the GST period, it is this account that should be used to code against either the 'refunds from' or 'payments to' the ATO that will appear on the bank statement. Xero has been designed to use only one GST account to track GST on income and expenses, so there is no need to add any new GST accounts to Xero.

Name: PAYG Withholdings Payable
Description: The amount of PAYE tax that is due to be paid

Name: Superannuation Payable
Description: The amount of superannuation that is due to be paid

Name: Income Tax Payable
Description: The amount of income tax that is due to be paid, also resident withholding tax paid on interest received.

Name: Franking Credits Attached to Dividends Received
Tax Credits
Description: Include any other tax credits withheld from gross income earned by the entity during the period.

Name: Historical Adjustment
Description: For accountant adjustments

Name: Loans from Beneficiaries/Settlors
Description: Include all advance/loans from beneficiaries or settlors, whether secured or unsecured, which are expected to be paid within 12 months of period end. If the loans are payable over a period extending beyond 12 months of period end, include only the portion that is expected to be repaid within 12 months of period end (known as the current portion).

Name: Secured Loans
Description: Secured loans are loans under which the entity (borrower) has pledged a specific asset or property which may be seized on default of loan obligations

Name: Unsecured Loans
Description: Unsecured loans often have higher interest rates and are supported by personal guarantees.

Name: Rounding
Description: An adjustment entry to allow for rounding

Name: Tracking Transfers
Description: Transfers between tracking categories

Name: Loans (NCL)
Description: Money that has been borrowed from a creditor

Name: Retained Earnings
Description: Do not Use

Name: Reserves
Description: Include all other reserves not otherwise separately categorised. These may include general reserves where the entity sets aside part of its retained earnings for a specified purpose (and hence the funds are unavailable for distribution).

Name: Foreign Currency Translation Reserve
Description: These are unrealised gains or losses resulting from the translation of financial reports at period end that are denominated in a currency other than the reporting currency. For example, this would arise when translating a branch or subsidiary''s financial statements into the reporting currency. This is quite different from the gains or losses resulting from the conversion of transactional items which are accounted for in the income statement.

Name: Revaluation Reserves
Description: A revaluation reserve records revaluations (unrealised gains) of non-current assets. Generally, this amount represents the difference between the market value of assets and their original cost.

Name: Units Held
Description: Units held ( if Unit trust) otherwise delete.

Name: Prior Period Losses Applied
Description: Journal out any prior period losses from "undistributed income" to this account

Name: Suspense
Description: An entry that allows an unknown transaction to be entered, so the accounts can still be worked on in balance and the entry can be dealt with later.
